---
title: "murals_exploration"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
# Load packages
library(tidyverse)
library(tidygeocoder)
library(janitor)
library(tidytext)
library(leaflet)
library(lubridate)
library(arcgisgeocode)

library(tigris)
library(sf)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```

## Street art !

```{r}
# Reading the now-comma-free headers + the rest and cleaning
murals <- read_csv("../data/Murals/streetartcities_US_2026-06.csv",
                   na = c("", "NA", "Unknown", "Untitled"))

murals <- murals %>% 
  mutate(across(32:ncol(murals), as.character)) %>% #otherwise it tries to parse them as lgl
  clean_names() #%>% # From janior package :)
```

```{r}
# Cleaning/wrangling further
murals_clean <- murals %>% 
  #select(where(function(x) !all(is.na(x)))) # could also do the ~ shorthand
  remove_empty("cols") %>% # From janitor package
  select(-country) %>% # It's US for all here
  select(1:attribute_last_seen) %>% # removing the rest of the cols since most are almost 100% NA and not really helpful for me
  mutate(description_lower = tolower(description)) # for text analysis? or do later
```

```{r}
# Using stringr+regex to fix the weird formatting
for(i in 1:ncol(murals_clean)){
  if(is.character(murals_clean[[i]])){
    murals_clean[[i]] <- str_remove_all(murals_clean[[i]], "<.*?>|\\[|\\]|\"")
  }
}
```

```{r}
# Text analysis of description

#region, state
#conservative vs liberal wrods/polarizing words
# west coast
# size of city

# Used claude for some help with the custom stopwords
custom_stop_words <- c(
  # Standard English
  "the", "of", "and", "a", "in", "to", "by", "is", "for", "on",
  "this", "s", "with", "that", "it", "was", "from", "as", "at",
  "i", "an", "are", "one", "has", "you", "all", "he", "my", "be",
  "their", "we", "who", "can", "her", "but", "out", "our", "they",
  "which", "so", "into", "have", "had", "an", "its", "been", "not",
  "also", "more", "up", "or", "about", "than", "when", "will",
  
  # Domain-specific (high-frequency, low signal)
  "mural", "murals", "art", "artist", "artists", "painted", "street",
  "wall", "walls", "photo", "work", "project", "city", 
  #"community",
  "building", "piece", "created", "located", "curated", "paint",
  "arts", "side", "part", "new", 
  #"local", 
  "life", 
  #"people",
  "through", "impermanent", # "impermanent" is included as domain-specific — it appeared 469 times, almost certainly from a boilerplate template phrase rather than meaningful content.
  
  # URL / web noise
  "https", "http", "com", "www", "instagram",
  
  # Emily's words
  NA, "nbsp", "project", "painting", "street", "NA", "san"
  
  # The places
  
)

# To use with tidytext:
 custom_df <- tibble(word = custom_stop_words, lexicon = "custom")
 stop_words_combined <- bind_rows(stop_words, custom_df)
# tokens |> anti_join(stop_words_combined, by = "word")



murals_description_words <- murals_clean %>% 
  #mutate(description_lower = str_remove_all(description_lower, "\\d+")) %>% # removing nums (like years, etc)
  unnest_tokens(output = word, input = description_lower) %>% 
  #anti_join(get_stopwords(source = "stopwords-iso"), by="word") %>% #could change the source...
  anti_join(stop_words_combined, by = "word") %>% 
  group_by(city_id) %>% 
  count(word) %>% 
  slice_max(n, n = 3)
```

## Group by region

```{r}
# Cache shapefiles so you don't have to re-download them
options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)

# Download cartographic boundary polygons for US Census Regions
region_polygons <- regions() %>% 
  st_transform(crs = 4326) # ensuring the same coordinate reference systems
```

```{r}
# Lil chart of regions
ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = region_polygons, aes(fill = NAME), color = "white")
```

```{r}
# https://sunsp.net/rspatial_book/spatialops.html 2.5
# Automatically filters murals inside polygons AND pulls over the "NAME" column
murals_clean <- st_as_sf(murals_clean, coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326)
murals_clean_regions <- st_join(murals_clean, region_polygons, join = st_within) %>%
  #filter(!is.na(NAME)) %>%       # Optional: removes murals that didn't fall into any region
  rename(region = NAME) %>% 
  select(-c(31, 32, 33, 35, 36, 37, 38))

# st_intersects could theoretically also work but it would include on teh borders
# st_contains would work if the two dfs were switched (bc a point can't contain a polygon)
```
